newsSinceJun6: No new fundamental disclosures since June 6. Price action is flow-driven: after a record high of Rs 684.45 on June 5, the stock froze at the 5% lower circuit on June 8 (Rs 588.40) alongside HFCL amid a global AI-infrastructure selloff and profit-taking, then bounced +4.2% to ~Rs 613 today on no news. The $1.1B multi-year hyperscaler PAL (May 22) and Neuralis AI data-center portfolio are pre-existing catalysts already in the price. On June 3 the exchange queried the volume spike and STL stated it was aware of no unpublished price-sensitive information.

sources.label: Business Standard - HFCL, Sterlite Tech freeze at 5% lower circuit after massive rally (Jun 8, 2026)
Business Standard - Sterlite Tech up 351% YTD, 5% upper circuit on $1bn AI order (May 25, 2026)
Upstox - India's AI proxy soars 450%: How Sterlite Tech outpaced Samsung and SK Hynix in 2026
Outlook Money - Sterlite Tech, HFCL tumble as global AI sell-off hits Indian data centre stocks

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict already demoted it to the bottom of the scaling list for post-rerating crowding; nothing genuinely new has emerged since, and today's +4.2% no-news bounce after Monday's circuit-freeze is retail flow, not evidence. Hold at rank 50 with mild negative drift - residual alpha after a +498% YTD run remains effectively zero.
verdicts.bull: The $1.1B FY27-FY29 hyperscaler PAL and 10x-36x fiber density per AI rack make STL a real, contracted beneficiary of AI data-center build-outs; order book grew from Rs 4,378 cr (FY25) to Rs 7,309 cr (FY26).
verdicts.neutral: The fiber-for-AI thesis is intact and now contractually validated, but at +498% YTD and a $3.2B cap the market has fully rerated it from hidden bottleneck to consensus AI proxy; the 14% June 5-8 drawdown only partially resets the setup.
verdicts.bear: Circuit-to-circuit retail churn, an exchange volume query, and a one-customer-concentrated $1B order leave huge air under the price; any hyperscaler capex pause or order-timing slip could unwind much of the 2026 move.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Additional hyperscaler PAL wins or Neuralis AI data-center order conversions that broaden the customer base beyond the single $1.1B award.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Momentum unwind in a crowded retail AI-proxy trade - further lower-circuit sessions if global AI-infra sentiment sours or the hyperscaler order's annual allocations disappoint.
